mission.title: lima: Unable to add hypervisor entitlement to qemu
mission.description: Unable to add hypervisor entitlement to qemu. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify lima troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your lima installation:
```bash
limactl --version
limactl list
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Confirm Unable to add hypervisor entitlement to qemu is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test lima to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Tests on multiple MacOS 10.15.7 and 11.3.1 machines showed that when this entitlement is added gatekeeper prevents qemu to start (error "kill -9"). I'm not entirely sure but I believe there was a breaking change from 10.15.6 to 10.15.7 and [Apple docs](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/bundleresources/entitlements/com_apple_security_hypervisor) are incorrect.

See discussion in #4
codeSnippets: Once I followed @christian-korneck 's https://github.com/AkihiroSuda/lima/issues/4#issuecomment-841578132, starting lima worked:

There's still some issues, that's probably due to my extract path (`/usr/local/opt/lima`)
I'm also having this issue. The workarounds mentioned haven't helped. I wonder if it's because SIP is enabled on my Mac?
> I wonder if it's because SIP is enabled on my Mac?

@irlevesque - the [above](https://github.com/AkihiroSuda/lima/issues/4#issuecomment-841578132) worked for me on macOS 11.3.1 (Intel), **with SIP enabled** (for completeness: I have dtrace allowed `csrutil enable --without dtrace`, but I don't think this matters here).

I also briefly tried to get it to work on github actions (which uses MacOS 10.15 on Intel) and I ran into "killed: 9" problems. Github Actions currently has an outage, so can't test any further today.
